seoTitle: Lock vs Lockleys — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lock and Lockleys. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lock and Lockleys.
heading: Lock vs Lockleys — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lock, South Australia and Lockleys, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lockleys has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Lock (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lock or Lockleys safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lock has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Lockleys scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lock scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Lockleys scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lock or Lockleys?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lockleys has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
